library(edgeR) # differential gene expression using neg.bin stats
## Loading required package: limma
## 
## Attaching package: 'limma'

##     plotMA
library(epiwraps)
## Loading required package: EnrichedHeatmap
## Loading required package: grid
## Loading required package: ComplexHeatmap

## Attaching package: 'epiwraps'
## The following object is masked from 'package:edgeR':
## 
##     getCounts
library(ggplot2)
library(sechm) # for plotting (features*samples) heatmaps
library(SummarizedExperiment)
#Data Extraction and Modification
##Download of the file
options(timeout = 3600)
dir.create("data")
## Warning in dir.create("data"): 'data' already exists
download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/mouse_mm38_hippocampus.peakCounts.SE.rds", destfile = "data/hippo_peaks.se.rds")
##Extract Peaks from Files
data_hippo<- readRDS("data/hippo_peaks.se.rds")
data_hippo$condition <- c("CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","FSS","FSS","FSS","FSS","FSS","FSS")
data_hippo$sex<- c("f","f","f","m", "m", "m", "f","f","f","m", "m", "m")

head(data_hippo)
## class: RangedSummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 6 12 
## metadata(0):
## assays(1): counts
## rownames: NULL
## rowData names(0):
## colnames(12): Control_female_GSM6041841.bam
##   Control_female_GSM6041842.bam ... FSS_male_GSM6041857.bam
##   FSS_male_GSM6041858.bam
## colData names(3): depth condition sex
#Standard Differential Analysis
##Comparing Forced Swim Stress vs. Control
dds_condition <- edgeR::DGEList(assay(data_hippo), group= data_hippo$condition)
dds_condition <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(dds_condition)
dds_condition <- edgeR::estimateDisp(dds_condition)
## Using classic mode.
et_condition<- as.data.frame(topTags(exactTest(dds_condition), Inf))
head(et_condition)
##           logFC   logCPM       PValue        FDR
## 15757  2.956480 5.568769 1.961625e-06 0.04052717
## 14137 -5.547319 7.661587 3.602032e-05 0.13176183
## 19358 -5.303583 6.889311 4.969718e-05 0.13176183
## 8714  -4.828259 8.216940 5.289166e-05 0.13176183
## 2118  -5.984659 8.519979 5.517843e-05 0.13176183
## 4967   2.858128 4.136854 5.948103e-05 0.13176183
ggplot(et_condition, aes(logFC, -log10(FDR))) + geom_point()

#Reporting of Condition Comparision
##Select the 10 most significant regions
###since topTags already sorts the regions by p.value, the 10 most significant regions are in rows 1-10
top10_condition<- head(et_condition, 10)
print(top10_condition)
##           logFC   logCPM       PValue        FDR
## 15757  2.956480 5.568769 1.961625e-06 0.04052717
## 14137 -5.547319 7.661587 3.602032e-05 0.13176183
## 19358 -5.303583 6.889311 4.969718e-05 0.13176183
## 8714  -4.828259 8.216940 5.289166e-05 0.13176183
## 2118  -5.984659 8.519979 5.517843e-05 0.13176183
## 4967   2.858128 4.136854 5.948103e-05 0.13176183
## 869   -4.385234 6.854042 6.447313e-05 0.13176183
## 12264 -4.818483 5.728855 7.544813e-05 0.13176183
## 3430  -4.283884 7.508608 7.707593e-05 0.13176183
## 17038 -4.973348 7.122513 9.351076e-05 0.13176183
#Heatmap of the 10 most significant regions
# top10_condition is a data.frame; extract its row names as a character vector
# Fix NULL rownames so sechm can match features
rownames(data_hippo) <- as.character(seq_len(nrow(data_hippo)))

# Add normalized logCPM assay
assay(data_hippo, "logcpm") <- log1p(cpm(dds_condition))

# Plot top 10 most significant regions
topRegions <- row.names(top10_condition)
sechm(data_hippo, topRegions, gaps_at = "condition")

##Comparing Forced between sex
dds_sex <- edgeR::DGEList(assay(data_hippo), group= data_hippo$sex)
dds_sex <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(dds_sex)
dds_sex <- edgeR::estimateDisp(dds_sex)
## Using classic mode.
et_sex<- as.data.frame(topTags(exactTest(dds_sex), Inf))
head(et_sex)
##          logFC   logCPM       PValue          FDR
## 20644 6.876619 6.682547 2.920870e-22 6.034518e-18
## 20643 6.864394 6.813826 3.240449e-19 3.347384e-15
## 20640 6.419731 5.522297 7.380945e-19 5.083011e-15
## 20647 6.184489 5.260409 4.044915e-17 2.089198e-13
## 20642 8.759947 4.548791 6.643047e-17 2.744907e-13
## 20648 7.175640 6.896536 9.138570e-17 2.984281e-13
ggplot(et_sex, aes(logFC, -log10(FDR))) + geom_point()

#Reporting of Sex Differences
top10_sex<- head(et_sex, 10)
print(top10_sex)
##           logFC   logCPM       PValue          FDR
## 20644  6.876619 6.682547 2.920870e-22 6.034518e-18
## 20643  6.864394 6.813826 3.240449e-19 3.347384e-15
## 20640  6.419731 5.522297 7.380945e-19 5.083011e-15
## 20647  6.184489 5.260409 4.044915e-17 2.089198e-13
## 20642  8.759947 4.548791 6.643047e-17 2.744907e-13
## 20648  7.175640 6.896536 9.138570e-17 2.984281e-13
## 17605 -5.905083 5.983284 1.011131e-16 2.984281e-13
## 17602 -5.473241 6.233290 1.249589e-16 3.031322e-13
## 20650  7.629468 6.982274 1.320518e-16 3.031322e-13
## 17604 -5.172471 6.575802 1.783856e-16 3.355202e-13
#Heatmap of the 10 most significant regions
# top10_sex is a data.frame; extract its row names as a character vector
# Fix NULL rownames so sechm can match features
rownames(data_hippo) <- as.character(seq_len(nrow(data_hippo)))

# Add normalized logCPM assay
assay(data_hippo, "logcpm") <- log1p(cpm(dds_sex))

# Plot top 10 most significant regions
topRegions <- row.names(top10_sex)
sechm(data_hippo, topRegions, gaps_at = "sex")
